title: Package Boundaries, Interfaces, Value Ownership, and Lifecycle · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for package boundaries, interfaces, value ownership, and lifecycle and accepts only compile and runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, parser, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for directed owned Go crawler graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, audit, store, report, and command dependencies inward through consumer-owned interfaces.
Specify ownership and copy rules for URLs, headers, byte slices, DOM selections, records, errors, and report rows crossing boundaries.
Define minimal interfaces at use sites and retain concrete constructors and lifecycle ownership at composition boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Error Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 9930 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove directed packages, value ownership, interfaces, resources, errors, and lifecycle closure; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.
